title: Beehiiv vs Brevo (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Beehiiv and Brevo email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which platform suits your needs best.
h1: Beehiiv vs Brevo: Detailed Comparison for 2025
intro: Beehiiv and Brevo are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different primary audiences. Beehiiv focuses on newsletter publishers seeking monetization and growth tools, while Brevo positions itself as an all-in-one CRM suite with multi-channel capabilities. This comparison will help you determine which platform best matches your business needs.

comparison_table.tool_a: Ad network, newsletter monetization, referral program
comparison_table.tool_b: SMS, WhatsApp, live chat, CRM, transactional API

choose_a_if: You're building a newsletter business and want monetization options through ads and sponsorships
You prefer a platform optimized specifically for email newsletters with a web reader feature
You value affiliate programs with generous commissions (50% first year)
choose_b_if: You need multi-channel marketing including SMS, WhatsApp, and live chat in one platform
You require transactional email API capabilities for automated customer communications
You want the most generous free tier with support for up to 100,000 subscribers at no cost

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your needs. Beehiiv excels for newsletter publishers seeking monetization and growth features, while Brevo is superior for businesses needing transactional email, SMS, and integrated CRM capabilities. Beehiiv has a higher G2 rating (4.7 vs 4.5), reflecting strong publisher satisfaction.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Beehiiv or Brevo?
faq.a: Brevo offers better value for most users. Its free tier supports 100,000 subscribers compared to Beehiiv's 2,500, and paid plans start at $25/month versus Beehiiv's $39/month. However, Beehiiv's pricing is more justifiable if you specifically need newsletter monetization features.

faq.q: Can I switch from Brevo to Beehiiv?
faq.a: Yes, switching is straightforward. Both platforms support CSV exports and API integrations, allowing you to migrate subscriber lists and campaign data. Plan your migration during a natural transition point and test imports thoroughly to ensure data integrity before fully switching.
